rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC A CALIFORNIAN CORP VS. MAHENDRA JHINGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 19, ASSIGNEE SACOR FINANCIAL, INC.'S Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption Hearing required. Judgment debtor is requested to bring documentation and/or be prepared to address the following: 1. whether the $200 for medical and dental payments (Item 4(e) in her financial statement) are duplicative of the $200 in installment payments shown on Item 4(j); 2. whether some or all of the medical and dental payments on the financial statement are duplicative of the "pre-tax medical" of $477.28 per month, shown on Item 2(b)(2) of the financial statement; 3. who is the debtor on the Department of Education debt of $8575.08 and 4. papers establishing that the $200 month on Item 4(e) is for co-payments for medical and dental visits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BH FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL PINKSTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF BH FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY'S MOTION For Order To Deem Matters Admitted Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THERE APPEARING TO BE NO OPPOSITION THE MOTION OF PLAINTIFF BH FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC IS GRANTED. COUNSEL WILL PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER, DELIVER TO DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT THE ORDER FOR SIGNATURE BY THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON APRIL 10, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: PHIL SIEGLE VS. STELLA STARR INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Add Judgment Debtor Ccp 187
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 4, ASSIGNEE RICHARD HYPPA Notice Of Motion To Add Judgment Debtor CCP 187 The motion is denied without prejudice. First, the motion confusingly refers to "Kats Resources" and "Kats Resources, Ltd." Second, plaintiff fails to present sufficient evidence showing that Mr. Yeh abused the corporate form, is the alter ego of the entities, or that he controlled the underlying litigation. See Triplett v. Farmer Ins. Exchange (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1415, 1421. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Stay Enforcement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT JACKSON JENKINS RENSTROM LLP'S Mtn To Stay Enforcement Denied. (CCP § 995.240.) Defendant law firm has not met its burden of showing that it is indigent and is unable to obtain sufficient sureties. The declarations of Ms. Coe and Ms. Brasch are conclusory and insufficient to warrant a stay of enforcement while Defendant appeals the judgment without the requisite undertaking required by CCP § 917.1. Defendant is an ongoing law firm with three offices in two states and about 60 employees. According to its firm administrator, it has "accounts receivable sufficient to cover its obligations" to its employees, vendors, and other creditors. Defendant has not met its burden of showing that it cannot find a way to make the cash deposit it states is necessary post a bond. There is nothing about the nature of this lawsuit (a breach of contract) which compels a contrary conclusion. Although Wells Fargo Bank is a private entity, there is no reason shown why it should be an unsecured creditor during the pendency of the appeal, particularly in light of Defendant law firm's statements that other creditors are being paid during the pendency of the appeal. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RIVER PROPERTY TRUST VS. DAWN WIGGINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted. Defendant shifted the burden that there was no triable issue of material fact regarding Plaintiff's breach of contract claim. Plaintiff does not dispute any of Defendant's evidence or raise a triable issue of material fact. Whether Plaintiff would have ever resubmitted the motion to the Bankruptcy Court and received approval by the Bankruptcy Court is speculative. Defendant's request for an order returning $10,000 to her is not part of this order granting summary judgment. Defendant did not move for summary judgment on her Cross-Complaint, and the Court cannot grant relief that is different from the grounds in the noticed motion. A determination of any awards of attorney fees and costs will be heard upon a properly filed motion by Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RYAN GARMAN et al VS. UNIVERSITY PIPELINE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft'S Supplemental Responses And Compliance With Pltfs' Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, And Deft Stephanie Losee'S Supplemental Responses And Compliance With Pltfs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF RYAN GARMAN, GARMAN GROUP, LLC Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft'S Supplemental Responses And Compliance With Pltfs' Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, And Deft Stephanie Losee'S Supplemental Responses And Compliance With Pltfs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (1) DEFENDANTS UNGER AND LOSEE WILL FILE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO ALL REQUESTS WHEREIN THEY RESPONDED WITH GENERAL OR SPECIFIC OBJECTIONS, BUT INDICATED: (A) A WILLINGNESS TO PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS; OR (B) THAT NO RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS EXIST. DEFENDANTS SHALL SPECIFICALLY STATE WHETHER OR NOT THEY ARE WITHHOLDING PRODUCTION OF ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS BASED UPON THESE OBJECTIONS. TO THE EXTENT THEY ARE, THEY WILL IDENTIFY SUCH DOCUMENTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW GARMAN TO FILE A MOTION CHALLENGING THE PROPRIETY OF WITHHOLDING SUCH DOCUMENTS. (2) THE PARTIES HAVE INDICATED A WILLINGNESS TO HAVE A CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT ENTERED INTO TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY PARTY BELIEVES THAT DOCUMENTS CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NOT SUBJECT TO PUBLIC DISCLOSURE. IT APPEARS FROM THE MATERIALS SUBMITTED TO THE COURT THAT THE PARTIES ARE FAIRLY CLOSE TO AN AGREEMENT ON THE FORM OF SUCH AN ORDER; TO THE EXTENT THAT IT CANNOT BE RESOLVED BY THE PARTIES THE COURT IS PREPARED TO CONSIDER AND ENTER A CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER SO THAT PRODUCTION CAN COMMENCE WITHIN 10 DAYS AFTER ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. (3) DEFENDANT UNGER'S OBJECTIONS TO SET ONE, REQUEST NOS. 14, 15, 16, 40, 41, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, 48, 54 & 55 AND SET TWO, REQUEST NOS. 90 -97 ARE SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT LOSEE'S OBJECTIONS TO THE SET TWO, REQUEST NOS. 90-97 ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARISE OUT OF HIS RELATIONSHIP WITH THE CORPORATE DEFENDANT AND BASED ON THE RECORD TO DATE THERE IS AN INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO INVADE DEFENDANTS' PRIVACY RIGHTS GUARANTEED BY CALIFORNIA LAW. MERE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE NOT A SUFFICIENT BASIS FOR REQUIRING THE REQUESTED PRODUCTION. SEE PART TWO OF TENTATIVE RULING

rulings.Case Title: ROGER L MEREDITH VS. JEFF GRIFFITHS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, And For Deemed Admissions, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ROGER MEREDITH Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, And For Deemed Admissions, And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT, JEFF GRIFFITH'S OBJECTIONS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 2.2, 2,5, 2.7, 12.1, 12.3 15.1, 17.1, 50.1 & 50.2 ARE OVERRULED AND DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO MAKE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO EACH OF THESE FORM INTERROGATORIES WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 3, 6, 10 & 12 DO NOT COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE APPLICABLE PROVISIONS OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE. WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DEFENDANT WILL PROVIDE EITHER AN ADMISSION, OR IF HE CONTINUES TO EITHER DENY OR CLAIM AN INABILITY TO EITHER ADMIT OR DENY, HE WILL PROVIDE ALL OF THE INFORMATION REQUIRED UNDER FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 AS TO WHY HE IS UNABLE TO ADMIT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSION ATTACHMENT 2 ARE OVERRULED AND DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER RESPONSES TO EACH OF THE REQUESTS 1-25 WITH EITHER AN ADMISSION OR A DENIAL AND IF NOT AN ADMISSION PROVIDE THE INFORMATION REQUIRED UNDER FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1. IF DEFENDANTS FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER AS TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THIS ORDER, ALL SUCH REQUESTS WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND THE GENUINENESS OF THE DOCUMENTS REFERENCED IN ATTACHMENT 2 WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED. MONTEREY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST DEFENDANT JEFF GRIFFITHS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,351.50 AND WILL BE PAID TO PLAINTIFF WITH 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON APRIL 10, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN CARTMELL et al VS. BITCOINICA LP, ALSO KNOWN AS BITCOINICA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT PATRICK STRATEMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action (negligence) on the ground of uncertainty. CCP §430.10(f). It is not certain whether Plaintiffs are alleging a cause of action for negligence or for breach of fiduciary duty, and the nature of the alleged wrongful act by Defendant Strateman under Civil Code §2343 or otherwise. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for conversion is overruled. Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled the elements of conversion, including the dollar amount of their claim. (Complaint 16). The analogy to a creditor-debtor relationship is distinguishable. (Cf. Metropolitan Life Ins. Co. v. San Francisco Bank (1943), 58 Cal. App. 2d 528.) The complaint alleges that Bitcoinica is a currency exchange. Complaint 10-14. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PAGE VS. MICHAEL WORNICK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 10, 2013, Line 17, CROSS DEFENDANT JOHN PAGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT The motion is denied. Plaintiff-Cross Defendant has not met its burden of showing that the cross-complaint, which seeks a declaration regarding plaintiff's insurance policy, arises from petitioning activity within the meaning of CCP 426.16 and the case law. See City of Cotati v. Cashman, (2002) 29 Cal.4th 69. The motion is frivolous, and Defendant has leave to file a motion for attorney's fees and costs. See CCP 425.16(c)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
